metadata.title: p53: The Cell Fate Decision Circuit
metadata.note: CLI video: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05-B08 slates · B09 outro

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

beats.role_note: the problem, the context, the stakes — BEFORE the CLI
beats.narration_text: p53 is the most commonly mutated gene across all human cancers. It does not function as a simple brake — it is a three-output decision circuit that determines the fate of every damaged cell.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'p53: most commonly mutated gene in cancer — not a brake, a three-output decision circuit'; no terminal yet

build.suggested: suggested prompt: p53 is the most commonly mutated gene across all human cancers. It does not function as a simple brake — it is a three-output decision circuit that determines t

beats.narration_text: Can Claude map the molecular logic of that decision?

props.command: claude "Map the molecular logic of the p53 decision circuit: three output pathways from p53 activation, the MDM2 negative feedback loop, and what happens when p53 is mutated in cancer. Include key molecular intermediaries."

beats.narration_text: Read the three branches: low damage routes through p21 and CDK2 inhibition. Sustained damage activates permanent senescence via p21 and RB. Severe damage triggers PUMA and NOXA — the intrinsic apoptosis pathway.

props.code:
# p53_circuit.py — Claude Code output
# p53 decision logic

# LOW DAMAGE:
#   p53 -> p21 -> inhibits CDK2 -> G1 arrest (transient)

# SUSTAINED DAMAGE:
#   p53 -> p21 + RB -> permanent senescence

# SEVERE DAMAGE:
#   p53 -> PUMA/NOXA -> BAX/BAK -> cytochrome c -> apoptosis

# MDM2 feedback (negative):
#   p53 activates MDM2 -> MDM2 ubiquitinates p53 -> p53 degraded
#   Nutlin-3 blocks MDM2 -> restores p53 in wt-p53 tumors

beats.narration_text: Three branches from a single node. Low damage routes to transient arrest. Sustained damage locks the cell into permanent senescence. Severe damage triggers apoptosis. MDM2 feeds back to degrade p53 — keeping the circuit from firing on minor stress.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: p53 decision tree with three outcome branches and MDM2 feedback loop

beats.narration_text: Low damage: p21 inhibits CDK2, pausing the cell cycle transiently. Sustained damage: permanent senescence. Severe damage: PUMA and NOXA trigger the intrinsic apoptosis pathway.

props.command: claude "Explain p21 and CDK2 interaction in G1 arrest, and how PUMA and NOXA activate BAX/BAK in the intrinsic pathway."

beats.narration_text: MDM2 creates a negative feedback loop — when p53 rises, MDM2 rises and degrades p53. This prevents runaway arrest under minor stress.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'MDM2 negative feedback — p53 activates MDM2 -> MDM2 degrades p53'; shows balance under low stress

build.suggested: suggested prompt: MDM2 creates a negative feedback loop — when p53 rises, MDM2 rises and degrades p53. This prevents runaway arrest under minor stress.

beats.narration_text: When cancer silences p53, it does not remove one brake. It removes the entire adjudication system.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'p53 loss = all three safeguards removed simultaneously: no transient arrest, no senescence, no apoptosis'

build.suggested: suggested prompt: When cancer silences p53, it does not remove one brake. It removes the entire adjudication system.

beats.narration_text: Therapeutic implication: MDM2 inhibitors like nutlin-3 restore p53 activity in tumors that retain wild-type p53 by blocking the MDM2 degradation signal.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 'MDM2 inhibitors (nutlin-3) -> block MDM2 ubiquitination -> restore p53 in wt-p53 tumors'

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Therapeutic implication: MDM2 inhibitors like nutlin-3 restore p53 activity in tumors that retain wild-type p53 by blocking the MDM2 degradation signal.

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
